object: ceramic table vase (solid of revolution, hollow)

scale.provenance: no scale cue in reference image; no known reference object in frame
scale.assumption: decorative ceramic table vase, typical overall height 250-350mm; built at range midpoint 300mm

parts.provenance: table surface the vase stands on; publishes a plane_z support (§13 PREVENT) so the vase's seating is a declared, measurable contact. Scale-invariant: sized relative to the vase footprint, not an absolute number (image pipeline).
averaged left+right silhouette from reference image via extract_profile.py; ogee_vase archetype confirmed by render-vs-reference loop; control points start and end on the revolve axis (C6)
solid of revolution: vase_profile spun 360 degrees about WorldZ, then capped into a closed solid (C6)
hollow the capped revolve solid into a thin-walled vessel, leaving the top face open (C6: cap before shell, closed brep required by Brep.CreateOffset solid=true)

binary_questions: Does the rendered silhouette read as a vase (wide belly, narrow neck) like the reference image?
Is the opening (top) hollow rather than a solid cap?
